title: Lastreopsis vogelii
intro: Lastreopsis vogelii is a species of fern native to the subtropical regions of Australia and New Zealand, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Lastreopsis vogelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lastreopsis vogelii is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections.
taxonomy: Lastreopsis vogelii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to Australia. The classification of Lastreopsis vogelii includes being a member of the genus Lastreopsis, which is a part of the family Dryopteridaceae. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly known as wood ferns. Lastreopsis vogelii is also classified as a part of the larger group known as the leptosporangiate ferns.
